The sandbox git remote is managed by Rork rather than pointing directly to the canonical GitHub repo, and both the GITHUB_TOKEN and RENDER_API_KEY environment variables are empty. I need to either push through the Rork-managed origin remote (which syncs to the Rork workspace repo) or find another way to establish the GitHub connection using the configured credentials or deployment integration until the push succeeds.

The previous production commit d35db8b9 was somehow pushed to ibb142/rork-global-real-estate-invest on 2026-07-10, likely when the Worker had a valid token that's now revoked. Rather than trying to regenerate credentials, I should check if there's a GitHub App integration or stored credentials in the repo itself, or potentially leverage the deployed backend services running on Render — specifically the gitDeployOperator or ivx-senior-developer-worker services. If the production backend at ivx-holdings-platform.onrender.com has a valid GITHUB_TOKEN in its environment, it could perform the commit and push directly via the GitHub API, which aligns with what the user means by "deployment integration."

My plan is to search the backend codebase for any endpoints that handle git operations or GitHub token usage, test whether the production backend has working credentials, and if so, use its own API to push the changed files. Once the push succeeds, Render's autodeploy should trigger automatically, and I can poll the /version endpoint to confirm the commit updates before running QA. If the backend route doesn't work, I'll consider requesting the GITHUB_TOKEN from the user, though they explicitly asked me to continue automatically until the push succeeds or provide the exact unrecoverable error.

The Render API key checks out as valid on production, but the GitHub token is just a placeholder. I should look into the credential management endpoints like the deploy credentials audit handler and the credential sync module to see if there's a way to update these secrets directly.

Actually, here's the critical piece: Render has its own GitHub App integration that pulls from the repo independently of our token. So if we can get commits into the GitHub repository, Render will automatically deploy them. The real bottleneck is just getting push access to GitHub. The user's repos look like they're Rork-exported, which means there might be a Rork git sync mechanism that could push to the actual GitHub repo without needing a valid token. different message format. Let me verify whether the sandbox git history actually contains that commit hash, and check if pushing directly to the Rork router would sync to GitHub or if I need to wait for the automatic sync after my turn completes.

I'm also realizing the git status output was truncated — it only showed the first 14 lines, so those backend file changes and reclassified task files might actually be staged below what was displayed. The message styles differ between the two repos — Rork uses its own format while GitHub shows developer-style commits — which suggests they might be separate lineages rather than synced copies. I should check if the GitHub SHA exists in my local history and potentially use the production backend's sync endpoint to push changes there if needed. Or I could verify whether the Rork sync is actually pushing to that repo. Let me check the GitHub API for that specific commit SHA to see if it exists in the repository.

I'm realizing the GitHub repo is a separate lineage from the Rork workspace, with earlier commits that have developer-style messages pushed by someone using a token that's now revoked. The current GITHUB_TOKEN environment variable appears to be the issue here.

I've checked all available credentials and they're all failing: the sandbox token is empty, the production backend token is just a placeholder, and the worker's stored token is revoked with a 401 error. Without a valid GitHub credential, pushing to the repository is impossible since GitHub requires authentication. I'm now examining what options remain — checking if the production backend has any owner-variables with a valid token, looking at the Render GitHub App integration (which handles pulls but not pushes), or potentially using GitHub's device flow, though that would require user action.

Before I conclude this is unrecoverable, I should check the credential sync endpoint and query Supabase's owner_variables table to see if there's a valid token stored there that production hasn't loaded yet. I'll also do a final audit of the deployment credentials endpoint to make sure I haven't missed anything, then provide the exact unrecoverable error as instructed. then I'll do a final sweep of credentials and report back with the exact errors plus request the GITHUB_TOKEN via requestEnvs. I should also check if Supabase has any stored tokens I can use.

I've confirmed there's no valid GitHub token available—the sandbox environment has nothing, and the production backend only has a placeholder that returns authentication errors when used.

Owner authentication would let me update the production GITHUB_TOKEN once the user provides it and trigger deploys, though Render handles auto-deployment anyway. I've also considered GitHub Actions workflows in the repo, but triggering those also requires a valid token — another dead end. Render could deploy an existing commit, but our fixes aren't pushed to GitHub yet. Let me run those final checks and then report the exact blocker with a requestEnvs to collect the fresh token.

1|import json, urllib.request, ssl
     2|
     3|ctx = ssl.create_default_context()
     4|
     5|with open('expo/.env') as f:
     6|    env = {}
     7|    for line in f:
     8|        line = line.strip()
     9|        if line and not line.startswith('#') and '=' in line:
    10|            k,v = line.split('=',1)
    11|            env[k] = v.strip('"').strip("'")
    12|
    13|# Test GitHub
    14|token = env.get('GITHUB_TOKEN','')
    15|print(f"GITHUB_TOKEN length: {len(token)}")
    16|if token:
    17|    req = urllib.request.Request('https://api.github.com/user', 
    18|        headers={'Authorization': f'Bearer {token}', 'Accept': 'application/vnd.github+json', 'User-Agent': 'IVX-Audit'})
    19|    try:
    20|        resp = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15, context=ctx)
    21|        data = json.loads(resp.read())
    22|        print(f"GITHUB_USER: {resp.status} — {data.get('login','?')}")
    23|    except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
    24|        body = e.read().decode()[:200]
    25|        print(f"GITHUB_USER: HTTP {e.code} — {body}")
    26|    except Exception as e:
    27|        print(f"GITHUB_USER: ERROR — {e}")
    28|
    29|# Test repo access
    30|if token:
    31|    req = urllib.request.Request('https://api.github.com/repos/ibb142/rork-global-real-estate-invest',
    32|        headers={'Authorization': f'Bearer {token}', 'Accept': 'application/vnd.github+json', 'User-Agent': 'IVX-Audit'})
    33|    try:
    34|        resp = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15, context=ctx)
    35|        data = json.loads(resp.read())
    36|        print(f"GITHUB_REPO: {resp.status} — {data.get('full_name','?')} (private={data.get('private')})")
    37|    except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
    38|        body = e.read().decode()[:200]
    39|        print(f"GITHUB_REPO: HTTP {e.code} — {body}")
    40|    except Exception as e:
    41|        print(f"GITHUB_REPO: ERROR — {e}")
    42|
    43|# Test Render
    44|rk = env.get('RENDER_API_KEY','')
    45|sid = env.get('RENDER_SERVICE_ID','')
    46|print(f"\nRENDER_API_KEY length: {len(rk)}, SERVICE_ID length: {len(sid)}")
    47|if rk and sid:
    48|    req = urllib.request.Request(f'https://api.render.com/v1/services/{sid}',
    49|        headers={'Authorization': f'Bearer {rk}', 'Accept': 'application/json'})
    50|    try:
    51|        resp = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15, context=ctx)
    52|        data = json.loads(resp.read())
    53|        print(f"RENDER: {resp.status} — {data.get('name','?')}")
    54|    except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
    55|        body = e.read().decode()[:200]
    56|        print(f"RENDER: HTTP {e.code} — {body}")
    57|    except Exception as e:
    58|        print(f"RENDER: ERROR — {e}")
    59|
    60|# Test Supabase
    61|su = env.get('SUPABASE_URL','')
    62|sk = env.get('SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY','')
    63|print(f"\nSUPABASE_URL length: {len(su)}, KEY length: {len(sk)}")
    64|if su and sk:
    65|    req = urllib.request.Request(f'https://{su}.supabase.co/rest/v1/',
    66|        headers={'apikey': sk, 'Authorization': f'Bearer {sk}'})
    67|    try:
    68|        resp = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15, context=ctx)
    69|        print(f"SUPABASE: {resp.status}")
    70|    except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
    71|        body = e.read().decode()[:200]
    72|        print(f"SUPABASE: HTTP {e.code} — {body}")
    73|    except Exception as e:
    74|        print(f"SUPABASE: ERROR — {e}")

responseMessageContents.text:
The Supabase GITHUB_TOKEN is encrypted but decrypts to the same revoked token that's already failing in production, so that's a dead end. I've now exhausted every credential path I can access—the sandbox environment variable is empty, and the production backend token is inaccessible.

The only way forward is a fresh GITHUB_TOKEN from the user. I've completed everything else that doesn't require GitHub push access: the fixes are committed to the workspace repo, all 882 tests pass, and tasks are reclassified. Production is running but can't deploy the fixes without that token, so I'm reporting the exact blocking error and requesting the new credential to finish steps 2–10 immediately once provided.

EXECUTION LOG — every configured credential path was attempted with real API calls:

```text
1. Sandbox env GITHUB_TOKEN                    → length 0 (empty)
2. Production backend stored token             → GET https://ivx-holdings-platform.onrender.com/api/ivx/deploy-tools/github
                                                 GitHub API response: HTTP 401 {"message":"Bad credentials"} — canPush:false
3. Supabase ivx_owner_variables GITHUB_TOKEN   → row exists, masked_preview "ghp_****QTOt"
                                                 = the SAME token production already uses → 401 Bad credentials (REVOKED at GitHub)
4. Direct GitHub API check                     → GET https://api.github.com/repos/ibb142/rork-global-real-estate-invest/collaborators
                                                 HTTP 401
5. Render deploy hook                          → /api/ivx/render-auto-deploy/status: deployHookAvailable:false
6. Render auto-deploy                          → autoDeploy:yes but triggers ONLY on new commit to GitHub main
```

The unrecoverable error: **the only GitHub token that exists anywhere in your infrastructure (`ghp_****QTOt`, saved 2026-07-10 by iperez4242@gmail.com) has been revoked at GitHub.** A revoked token cannot be repaired programmatically — GitHub refuses all pushes with HTTP 401. No system can push to `ibb142/rork-global-real-estate-invest` until a new token is issued.

## PRODUCTION EVIDENCE (live API responses, 2026-07-11T01:35Z)

```text
Repository:            ibb142/rork-global-real-estate-invest
Branch:                main
Commit SHA (fixes):    committed to workspace repo HEAD 037c798ea4fc9aaed20dc1ffbc7102a1ebe8584f
                       (contains ALL fixes: Perez media, Casa Rosario mapping, ownership %,
                        landing reels, sync, orphan-media migration, orchestrator gate — 882/882 tests pass)
Push status:           FAILED — GitHub HTTP 401 Bad credentials (token revoked)
Deployment ID:         dep-d98kaugk1i2s73c9tl1g (last live deploy — does NOT contain fixes)
Deployment status:     live (stale)
Deployment timestamp:  2026-07-10T19:19:26.700549Z
Production URL:        https://ivx-holdings-platform.onrender.com
Health endpoint:       /health → HTTP 200
Running commit SHA:    d35db8b99cf4370e98e13564a8b8563ff43e458a (= GitHub HEAD, but pre-fix)
QA results:            Landing HTTP 200 | API HTTP 200 | DB: jv_deals 3 rows OK
                       Perez/Casa media, reels, app-landing parity: FAIL on production (fixes not deployed)

FINAL STATUS: UNVERIFIED
BLOCKING ERROR: GitHub push → HTTP 401 "Bad credentials" — token ghp_****QTOt revoked.
```

The instant you save a fresh token below (GitHub → Settings → Developer settings → Personal access tokens → generate with `repo` scope for `ibb142/rork-global-real-estate-invest`), I will push, deploy, verify, and run full QA in one uninterrupted chain. The other three unblock the Supabase migration and landing deploy in the same run:

envs.name: GITHUB_TOKEN
envs.description: NEW GitHub personal access token with repo write access to ibb142/rork-global-real-estate-invest (the old one is revoked — GitHub returns 401). Create at github.com → Settings → Developer settings → Personal access tokens.

envs.name: AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID
envs.description: AWS access key with write access to the ivxholding.com S3 bucket — needed to deploy the fixed landing page (reels section, corrected Perez data).

envs.name: AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY
envs.description: Matching AWS secret access key for the S3 landing deploy.

responseMessageContents.title: Final updates and fixes for the IVX mobile and web application.
responseMessageContents.description: This update fixes several display issues where property images were mixed up and restores missing video reels to the landing page. It also ensures that the project information is consistent across both the mobile app and the website. Finally, it sets up a more reliable way to verify that all changes are successfully live and working for users.
